title: Cherry Balsamic Pork Tenderloin with Thyme Potatoes
description: HelloFresh Hall of Fame — perfectly seared pork tenderloin glazed with a rich cherry balsamic reduction and caramelized onions, served alongside crispy thyme-roasted potatoes and broccoli. Elegant enough for a special occasion, easy enough for a weeknight.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Cut potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Toss with 1½ tbsp olive oil, thyme leaves (stripped from 2 sprigs), salt, and pepper. Roast on a baking sheet for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway.
Add broccoli to the potato pan for the last 15 minutes of roasting, tossed with a little olive oil and salt.

Pat pork tenderloin dry and season generously with salt and pepper. Heat 1½ tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear pork on all sides, 2–3 minutes per side, until golden brown.
Transfer skillet to oven and roast pork for 12–15 minutes until internal temperature reaches 145°F. Remove pork and let rest on a cutting board.
Return skillet to medium heat. Add butter and sliced onion; cook 5 minutes until softened and golden. Add garlic and remaining thyme; cook 1 minute.
Add cherry preserves, balsamic vinegar, and chicken broth. Simmer 3–4 minutes, stirring, until the glaze thickens.
Slice pork tenderloin into medallions. Arrange on plates with thyme potatoes and broccoli. Spoon cherry balsamic glaze and caramelized onions generously over the pork.
tips: Don't skip the resting time — letting the pork rest 5 minutes after roasting keeps it juicy.
Pork tenderloin cooks fast; use a meat thermometer to avoid overcooking. 145°F is perfect — slightly pink in the center is safe and delicious.
Cherry jam, preserves, or even dried cherries rehydrated in a little water all work for the glaze.
The glaze is also excellent on chicken thighs or duck breast.
